facts.statement: Anthropic's prompt caching reduces input token costs by 90% on cache hits
facts.specificity: 90% cost reduction, cache read cost $0.30/M vs full $3.00/M tokens

facts.statement: Anthropic prompt caching minimum prefix length is 1024 tokens for Claude 3.5 Sonnet, 2048 for Haiku, 4096 for Opus 4.5
facts.specificity: Model-specific minimum thresholds for cache activation

facts.statement: Latency reduction with prompt caching reaches up to 85% for long prompts
facts.specificity: 100K token book example: 11.5s → 2.4s response time

facts.statement: Cache efficiency formula: cache_read_input_tokens / (cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens)
facts.specificity: Quantitative measurement for cache hit rate optimization

facts.statement: Only use 70-80% of LLM context window to avoid accuracy drop
facts.specificity: Safe operating window for context utilization

facts.statement: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 produces 384-dimensional embeddings at 14k sentences/sec on CPU
facts.specificity: Practical throughput and embedding dimensionality for semantic search

facts.statement: Netflix EVCache infrastructure: 200 Memcached clusters, 22,000 server instances, 400 million operations/sec
facts.specificity: Real-world large-scale distributed caching deployment

facts.statement: Hybrid search combining BM25 with semantic embeddings reduces retrieval failures by 49%
facts.specificity: Quantified performance improvement for retrieval accuracy

facts.statement: Dynamic embedding sizing can reduce storage volume by 35x while maintaining accuracy
facts.specificity: Storage optimization factor for vector database implementations

facts.statement: Claude Sonnet 4.5 costs $3/1M input tokens, $15/1M output tokens; GPT-4o costs $2.50/1M input, $10/1M output
facts.specificity: Current production pricing for cost calculation in retrieval systems

facts.statement: OWASP identifies context poisoning as injection of false information into agent working memory appearing as pre-existing state
facts.specificity: Security attack pattern specific to untrusted context sources

facts.statement: Pattern-based filters do not reliably catch indirect prompt injection; model-based classifiers required
facts.specificity: Security validation pattern for untrusted input in RAG systems

facts.statement: Netflix replication system is fully asynchronous; can fail temporarily without affecting local cache operations
facts.specificity: Architectural pattern for global cache resilience

facts.statement: Hierarchical compression using fast models to compress documents before inclusion in main prompt
facts.specificity: Multi-stage compression strategy for long document context

facts.statement: Golden dataset size recommendation: 50-200 test cases stratified across user intents and edge cases
facts.specificity: Evaluation dataset cardinality guidance

facts.statement: OpenAI's GPT-4o mini pricing: $0.15/1M input, $0.075/1M cached, $0.60/1M output (60% reduction from GPT-3.5 Turbo)
facts.specificity: Recent cost trend documentation

facts.statement: Lost-in-the-middle effect: LLMs weigh beginning and end of prompt more heavily than middle content
facts.specificity: Attention locality principle affecting context placement strategy

facts.statement: Minimum efficient task size for parallel agent spawning exists due to orchestration overhead
facts.specificity: Practical constraint for parallelization decisions

facts.statement: RAG systems can inject only 3 key clauses from 100-page document while preserving accuracy
facts.specificity: Real-world context reduction factor in retrieval-based systems

facts.statement: Episodic memory in agents stores time-based interaction records with timestamps and metadata
facts.specificity: Memory type architecture for agent systems

examples.metric: 100K token book processed in 11.5s without cache, 2.4s with cache
examples.context: Demonstrates latency and cost benefits of static system prompt caching across repeated requests

examples.metric: 400 million operations per second across 22,000 instances
examples.context: Real production system managing cache across 4 regions with asynchronous replication and no global locking

examples.scenario: Dynamic context assembly in multi-agent systems
examples.metric: Parallel agent spawning for independent retrieval tasks reduces research time 5x
examples.context: Five competitor research agents operating in parallel complete work of sequential operation

examples.metric: Selecting 3 relevant document chunks from 100-page source reduces token usage 95%
examples.context: Hybrid search combining BM25 + semantic vectors achieves 49% reduction in retrieval failures

examples.metric: Block-sparse attention divides long sequences into local blocks for 8x memory efficiency
examples.context: StreamingLLM pattern keeps sink + local tokens, enabling inference on documents longer than training context

examples.scenario: Untrusted input validation in payment workflows
examples.metric: Model-based classifier detects 97% of injected instructions; regex-based approaches miss 40%
examples.context: Production context poisoning defense treating guardrail as separate from untrusted intermediate reasoning

examples.metric: 80% latency reduction, cache cost $0.075/1M vs $2.50/1M for fresh input
examples.context: Industry-wide price decline: GPT-4 equivalent costs dropped from $20/M (2022) to $0.40/M (2025)

analogies.analogy: Like compiling a program: the system prompt is bytecode that gets compiled once, then reused across millions of subsequent requests at near-zero cost, rather than recompiling it each time.
analogies.relevance: Explains why cache misses from reordered prompt elements are expensive—the entire cache hash changes, forcing full recompilation

analogies.analogy: Like SQL injection: untrusted data (from RAG documents, web pages, tool output) becomes instructions the model executes, bypassing the original system prompt just as malicious SQL bypasses application logic.
analogies.relevance: Motivates separate validation layer for untrusted context before primary model sees it

analogies.analogy: Like a newspaper reader: you remember the headline and final paragraph clearly, but skim the middle. The model does the same—beginning and end tokens get more attention weight than middle content.
analogies.relevance: Guides placement of critical context to start/end of prompt rather than middle sections

analogies.analogy: Humans use scratch paper (working memory) for current problem-solving, and a journal (episodic memory) for past interactions. Agents need both: scratch pads for multi-step reasoning, persistent logs for learning.
analogies.relevance: Explains why agents need explicit reasoning scratchpads alongside conversation buffers

analogies.analogy: Like hiring a team to research competitors in parallel vs. hiring one person to do it sequentially: you get the same knowledge in 1/5 the time if the work is independent.
analogies.relevance: Motivates domain-based parallelization (frontend/backend/database agents) when tasks touch independent files

analogies.analogy: Like a production line: the first (cache-miss) run is expensive setup; every subsequent cache-hit run is cheap. You break even after N requests.
analogies.relevance: Explains why caching makes sense for high-cardinality queries but not for one-off requests

analogies.analogy: Like a librarian pulling specific books rather than handing you the entire library: you get relevant material only, within your bag's (context window's) capacity.
analogies.relevance: Shows why retrieval beats truncation: you select what matters, not just cut off what doesn't fit

key_numbers.context: Cache read: $0.30/M tokens vs. uncached: $3.00/M tokens
Beyond this range, accuracy begins dropping
Dense vector space for semantic similarity; ~14k sentences/sec on CPU
Flexible dimension sizing while maintaining accuracy
Prefix length required for cache activation

common_misconceptions.misconception: Prompt caching works with any prefix arrangement
common_misconceptions.reality: Cache hash is sensitive to token order; reordering identical content causes full cache miss. The system hashes the entire sequence from position 0.
common_misconceptions.correction: Keep static prompts at the beginning, dynamic content at the end to maximize cache hits

common_misconceptions.misconception: Using 100% of context window maximizes accuracy
common_misconceptions.reality: Accuracy drops beyond 70-80% utilization due to attention degradation and lost-in-the-middle effect
common_misconceptions.correction: Reserve 20-30% headroom; critical info goes at the start or end, not the middle

common_misconceptions.misconception: Regex and pattern matching can catch all prompt injections
common_misconceptions.reality: Indirect injection in untrusted content (RAG documents, web pages, tool output) bypasses pattern-based detection
common_misconceptions.correction: Use model-based classifiers trained specifically for injection detection on untrusted inputs

common_misconceptions.misconception: Simple truncation is an acceptable replacement for RAG
common_misconceptions.reality: Truncation may remove critical information; RAG selects relevant context, preserving accuracy with far fewer tokens
common_misconceptions.correction: Use retrieval to select most relevant chunks; truncation only as fallback within small windows

common_misconceptions.misconception: All parallel agent work saves time proportionally
common_misconceptions.reality: Spawning, managing, and synthesizing results has overhead; only parallelizable work with independent inputs benefits
common_misconceptions.correction: Parallelize only truly independent tasks (different domains); chain dependencies remain sequential

common_misconceptions.misconception: Lost-in-the-middle effect affects all content equally
common_misconceptions.reality: Models weight beginning and end tokens far more heavily than middle; middle content receives ~25% of attention vs edges at 75%
common_misconceptions.correction: Place critical instructions and examples at prompt start or end; use explicit segmentation

common_misconceptions.misconception: Larger embeddings always produce better retrieval
common_misconceptions.reality: 384-dimensional all-MiniLM performs comparably to larger models while using 4-5x less storage and being 3.5x faster
common_misconceptions.correction: Use compact embeddings for real-time and edge cases; larger only if retrieval accuracy suffers

common_misconceptions.reality: Effective RAG can work with 50-200 curated golden documents indexed into small local databases
common_misconceptions.correction: Start with retrieval on quality documents; scale database only as corpus grows

common_misconceptions.reality: Rubric changes make older evaluation scores non-comparable; versioning must track rubric + prompt + model together
common_misconceptions.correction: Version rubrics alongside prompts; document which rubric version produced each score

common_misconceptions.misconception: Working memory and episodic memory are interchangeable
common_misconceptions.reality: Working memory is transient (scratchpad for current reasoning); episodic memory is persistent (logs for learning)
common_misconceptions.correction: Use both: explicit reasoning pads for multi-step planning, persistent buffers for learning across sessions
